blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: okay so I have to say something

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: that girl has been patient bro. she texted you after a rough day, she asked about you, and THEN she asked about the movies. that's not random. she planned that.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: no cap. she was waiting for the right moment to ask. and she found it.
I read the last message. Then I read Crystal's texts again from the beginning. He wasn't wrong.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: good. means you care. just don't walk in trying to be smooth. just be you.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: you sound like you've been preparing that speech

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: dead serious I have. I been waiting for you to figure out a reason to go.
Friday at school. I saw Crystal in the hallway between second and third period.
She was walking with a girl I didn't recognize. She looked up when I passed. I nodded. She nodded back. That was it.
There was no text thread. No context to do anything with a look in a hallway. Just — a look. And then we were both somewhere else.
I kept walking. Third period. Ms. Barrett. A writing prompt I mostly stared at.
I thought about asking for her number. The moment on the steps at the end of Episode 2. What I did and what I didn't. That decision had a shape now. I could feel the edges of it.
